1
True/False
CORRECT
1/1
Grade: 1 out of 1 point possible

Active recall is more effective when learners retrieve information before checking notes.

    TrueCorrect answer
    False

Question 2
2
Multiple Choice
CORRECT
1/1
Grade: 1 out of 1 point possible

Which approach best supports long-term retention?

    Option A

    Rereading the same paragraph several times in one sitting

    Option B

    Highlighting every sentence in a chapter

    Option C

    Correct:

    Spacing review sessions across multiple days
    Correct answer

    Option D

    Studying only on the night before an exam

Question 3
3
Multiple Choice
CORRECT
1/1
Grade: 1 out of 1 point possible

What is the main benefit of summarizing a section in your own words?

    Option A

    It replaces the need to check accuracy

    Option B

    Correct:

    It reveals whether the idea was understood well enough to explain
    Correct answer

    Option C

    It guarantees every detail has been memorized

    Option D

    It makes diagrams unnecessary

    Option E

    It removes the need for later review

Question 4
4
True/False
CORRECT
1/1
Grade: 1 out of 1 point possible

A useful study plan should avoid breaks so concentration never resets.

    True
    FalseCorrect answer

Question 5
5
Multiple Choice
CORRECT
1/1
Grade: 1 out of 1 point possible

When reviewing missed questions, what should be saved with the answer?

    Option A

    Only the final score

    Option B

    Correct:

    The reason the selected answer was wrong
    Correct answer

    Option C

    The color of the original worksheet

    Option D

    The number of minutes until the next class
